  "network_summary": "42 validators scored. A critical data limitation is that ALL validators have null ASN and null geolocation. This prevents any meaningful geographic or infrastructure diversity assessment, resulting in a uniform diversity penalty (score 20) for all candidates. Consequently, scores are driven almost entirely by Consensus Performance, Operational Reliability (UNL/Domain), and Identity. \n\nConsensus Health: The network is split between high-performing validators (>99% 30d agreement) and a small group of severely underperforming ones. v006 and v010 are effectively offline (0% recent agreement). v036 and v035 show poor long-term agreement (<95%), likely due to instability or misconfiguration. \n\nReliability & Identity: Five validators (v002, v016, v032, v038, v039) are on the UNL and show perfect consensus, earning the highest scores (88). Three validators (v004, v015, v028) have verified domains, boosting their identity/reliability scores despite not being on the UNL. Most other validators lack domain verification or have no domain, capping their reliability/identity scores. \n\nDiversity: Due to the null geolocation data, no diversity bonuses were applied. In a production environment with resolved IPs, validators in underrepresented regions would receive significant boosts. Here, the lack of data is treated as a risk/unknown, penalizing the diversity dimension uniformly."
}

scores_by_validator_id.network_summary:
42 validators scored. A critical data limitation is that ALL validators have null ASN and null geolocation. This prevents any meaningful geographic or infrastructure diversity assessment, resulting in a uniform diversity penalty (score 20) for all candidates. Consequently, scores are driven almost entirely by Consensus Performance, Operational Reliability (UNL/Domain), and Identity. 

Consensus Health: The network is split between high-performing validators (>99% 30d agreement) and a small group of severely underperforming ones. v006 and v010 are effectively offline (0% recent agreement). v036 and v035 show poor long-term agreement (<95%), likely due to instability or misconfiguration. 

Reliability & Identity: Five validators (v002, v016, v032, v038, v039) are on the UNL and show perfect consensus, earning the highest scores (88). Three validators (v004, v015, v028) have verified domains, boosting their identity/reliability scores despite not being on the UNL. Most other validators lack domain verification or have no domain, capping their reliability/identity scores. 

Diversity: Due to the null geolocation data, no diversity bonuses were applied. In a production environment with resolved IPs, validators in underrepresented regions would receive significant boosts. Here, the lack of data is treated as a risk/unknown, penalizing the diversity dimension uniformly.
